perturbation.original_text: Licensee Know-how shall mean with respect to the Licensed Product (as defined hereunder) all know-how, trade secrets and scientific, technical, including manufacturing or regulatory information, developed, applied or acquired by Eurofarma which relates to the identification, characterization, expression, synthesis, use, or production of the Licensed Product and which is reasonably useful or necessary to research, develop, use, make, have made, import, distribute, offer for sale, and/or sell the Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee Know-how shall mean with respect to the Licensed Product (as defined hereunder) all know-how, trade secrets and scientific, technical, including manufacturing or regulatory information, developed, applied or acquired by Eurofarma which relates to the identification, characterization, expression, synthesis, use, or production of the Licensed Product and which is reasonably useful or necessary to research, develop, use, make, have made, import, distribute, offer for sale, and/or sell the Licensed Product. For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee Know-how shall be owned by Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that Licensee Know-how is owned by the Licensor. This contradicts the original understanding that Licensee Know-how belongs to Eurofarma, and in particular, it contradicts clause 13 'Licensee Know-how shall belong to the Licensee.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee Know-how shall belong to the Licensee. The Licensee hereby grants to the Licensor an irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, worldwide license to use of any Licensee Know-how.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states 'Licensee Know-how shall be owned by Licensor,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text: 'Licensee Know-how shall belong to the Licensee.' This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding ownership of the know-how, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: NLS will supply Eurofarma with the finished Licensed Product based on its COGS.
perturbation.changed_text: NLS will supply Eurofarma with the finished Licensed Product at a price solely determined by NLS, irrespective of COGS or market value. The Parties will enter in due time into a separate manufacturing and supply agreement concerning the Licensed Product, with the possibility of tech-transfer during the term of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The altered clause creates a contradiction regarding pricing. Originally, the price was based on COGS. The change now states the price is solely determined by NLS, removing any reference to COGS or market value. This contradicts with the original text and may impact commercialization in article 11 which states 'Subject to Article 10.3 above, Eurofarma shall be in charge and solely responsible for any pre-marketing, marketing, selling, warehousing, handling, distributing and all other commercial activities in relation to the Licensed Product in the countries of the Territory and these activities shall be determined by Eurofarma at its sole discretion and expenses, provided that Eurofarma shall use commercially reasonable efforts to commercialize the Licensed Product in the Lead Countries as a priority and in the other countries of the Territory.'. Eurofarma being in charge of commercial activities will now be affected by the price being solely detemined by NLS.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Article 10.3 above, Eurofarma shall be in charge and solely responsible for any pre-marketing, marketing, selling, warehousing, handling, distributing and all other commercial activities in relation to the Licensed Product in the countries of the Territory and these activities shall be determined by Eurofarma at its sole discretion and expenses, provided that Eurofarma shall use commercially reasonable efforts to commercialize the Licensed Product in the Lead Countries as a priority and in the other countries of the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that NLS solely determines the price of the Licensed Product, irrespective of COGS or market value. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Eurofarma is solely responsible for commercial activities, implying they have control over pricing within the territory. NLS setting the price independently significantly undermines Eurofarma's commercial control and potentially their ability to effectively manage marketing, selling, and distribution, thus creating a direct and impactful contradiction. The original text stated the price was based on COGS, giving Eurofarma more leeway within their commercial activities, but the Changed Text removes this pricing structure, and gives full authority to NLS.

perturbation.original_text: Such records shall be retained by the Licensee for ten (10) years following a given reporting period.
perturbation.changed_text: Such records shall be retained by the Licensee for five (5) years following a given reporting period; however, if NLS requests in writing, the records must be destroyed immediately.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction regarding how long the records must be retained. The original text states ten years, while the change says five years with a condition of immediate destruction if requested by NLS. This affects the record and audit obligations listed in article 3.5 where the change creates ambiguity and the records can not be audited because they were destroyed immediately.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Records and Audits The Licensee shall keep, and shall require its Affiliates and Distributors to keep, accurate and correct records of the Licensed Product used and sold under this Agreement. Such records shall be retained by the Licensee for ten (10) years following a given reporting period. Subject to a written advance notice of thirty (30) calendar days, all relevant records supporting the preparation of the Reports shall be made available during normal business hours for inspection at the expense of Licensor by Licensor or by a selected representative of Licensor for the sole purpose of verifying the Reports and the accuracy of the payments made or due to Licensor under this Agreement. Such inspector shall not request any other documents or information other than these related to this purpose as determined in its sole discretion and the Licensee shall have no obligation to provide the inspector or Licensor any documents or information not related thereto. In the event that any such inspection shows an underreporting and underpayment by the Licensee to Licensor under the terms of this Agreement in excess of one percent (1%) for any twelve-month (12-month) period, then Licensee shall pay the cost of the audit as well as any additional sum that would have been payable to Licensor had the Licensee reported correctly, plus an interest charge at a rate of ten percent (10%) per year. Such interest shall be calculated from the date the correct payment was due to Licensor up to the date when such payment is actually made by the Licensee. For underpayment not in excess of one percent (1%) for any twelve-month (12-month) period, the Licensee shall pay the difference within thirty (30) days without having to pay for the inspection cost but with interest charge calculated as per the provisions of this Article.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 10-year record retention period. The changed text reduces this to 5 years, with a clause allowing for immediate destruction upon NLS request. This directly contradicts the 'Records and Audits' section requiring the Licensee to keep records for 10 years. The immediate destruction clause further undermines the audit process outlined in the contradicted text, as records may not exist for the full 10-year period required for audit purposes, creating a structural flaw and inconsistency.
